# Vibe Test — Complete Reference for LLMs

## Overview

Vibe Test is a code-aware browser testing agent distributed as an npm package (`vibe-test`). It provides an MCP server with 11 tools that let AI code editors (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf) autonomously test web applications.

The core philosophy: **the editor LLM is the brain, Vibe Test is the hands.** Each tool returns structured JSON data plus screenshots as base64 images, allowing the LLM to see the page and reason about it visually.

## Installation

```bash
# As MCP server (recommended — add to editor's mcp.json)
{ "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "vibe-test@latest", "--mcp"] }

# As CLI
npx vibe-test run https://your-app.com --codebase /path/to/project

# As npm package
npm install -g vibe-test
```

## Complete Tool Reference

### scan_codebase
**Must be called first.** Analyzes the project source code:
- Detects framework (React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Svelte, etc.)
- Parses all routes (file-based and code-defined)
- Extracts forms, buttons, dialogs, CRUD operations per route
- Reads existing Playwright/Jest/Cypress test files
- Maps test coverage and identifies gaps
- Generates test scenarios for uncovered features

Parameters:
- `codebase_path` (string, optional): Absolute path to project root. Defaults to cwd.
- `url` (string, optional): App URL to test against.
- `mode` (string, optional): "fast" or "deep". Default "deep".

Returns: JSON with routes, features, coverage gaps, generated scenarios.

### login
Authenticates in a real Playwright browser:
- Navigates to login page
- Fills email/password fields
- Clicks submit
- Waits for redirect
- Captures localStorage tokens and cookies
- Returns screenshot of post-login state

Parameters:
- `email` (string): Login email
- `password` (string): Login password
- `login_url` (string, optional): Login page path. Default "/login".

Returns: JSON with success status, tokens, API calls + screenshot.

### scan_page_elements
Discovers all interactive elements on a page without clicking them:
- Buttons, links, inputs, selects, checkboxes, textareas, tabs
- For each: tag, type, text, selector, disabled state, href

Parameters:
- `route` (string): Page route to scan
- `authenticated` (boolean, optional): Use authenticated context. Default false.

Returns: JSON element list + page screenshot.

### explore_page
Full interactive exploration — acts like a senior QA tester:
- Clicks every button and observes state changes
- Tests every link and verifies navigation
- Fills inputs with appropriate test data
- Monitors all API calls (excluding static assets)
- Records success/failure for each interaction

Parameters:
- `route` (string): Page route to explore
- `authenticated` (boolean, optional): Use authenticated context. Default false.

Returns: JSON with interaction results, API calls, timings + screenshot.

### execute_scenario
Runs a specific multi-step test scenario:
- Navigate, fill, click, wait, assert actions
- Step-by-step execution with screenshots
- Heuristic verification of outcomes
- Reuses existing browser context (fast)

Parameters:
- `scenario` (object): Scenario with id, name, route, steps[], expected_outcome
- `authenticated` (boolean, optional): Default false.

Returns: JSON with status (pass/fail/error), step logs, screenshots.

### get_coverage
Returns test coverage analysis from the last scan_codebase call:
- Routes with test counts
- Coverage gaps with priority levels
- Available scenarios to run

Returns: JSON with coverage map, gaps, suggested scenarios.

### suggest_tests
Intelligent gap analysis that returns executable scenarios:
- Identifies untested CRUD operations (critical priority)
- Finds missing form validation tests (high priority)
- Surfaces broken elements from exploration (high priority)
- Suggests retests for previously failing routes (high priority)
- Flags API errors found during exploration (critical/high)
- Lists untested navigation flows (low priority)

Parameters:
- `route` (string, optional): Focus on specific route. Omit for all routes.

Returns: JSON with prioritized suggestions, each containing an executable scenario object.

### take_screenshot
Quick visual verification:
- Navigates to URL
- Waits for page load
- Captures screenshot

Parameters:
- `url` (string): Full URL or route path
- `authenticated` (boolean, optional): Default false.
- `full_page` (boolean, optional): Full page or viewport. Default false.

Returns: Screenshot.

### generate_report
Generates interactive HTML report:
- All test results with pass/fail status
- Step-level screenshots with click-to-enlarge
- Page exploration findings
- API monitoring results
- Coverage gap suggestions
- Auto-opens in browser

Parameters:
- `title` (string, optional): Report title.

Returns: Report path + summary.

### run_full_test
All-in-one orchestration:
- scan_codebase → generate scenarios → execute all → explore pages → generate report

Parameters:
- `url` (string): App URL
- `codebase_path` (string, optional): Project root path
- `mode` (string, optional): "fast" or "deep"
- `headed` (boolean, optional): Show browser. Default true.

Returns: Full results + report.

### cleanup
Closes all browsers and resets session state. Call when done testing.

## VIBE.md Project Guidance

Projects can include a `VIBE.md` file to guide testing:

```markdown
## Login URL
/login

## Test Credentials
- Email: test@example.com
- Password: TestPass123!

## Never Automate
- delete account
- cancel subscription

## Known Flaky
- /notifications

## Notes
- Profile page requires clicking "Edit Profile" first
```

## Self-Improvement

Vibe Test persists intelligence in `.vibe/intel.json`:
- Working selectors per route
- Route load timings
- Auth state and credentials
- Failure patterns and flaky route tracking
- Run history with pass rate trends

Each subsequent run uses this intelligence to be faster and more accurate.
